statement: Never use heredocs, long inline interpreter scripts (node -e, python -c, bash -c with multi-line bodies), or shell redirection file writes (>, >>, | tee) in agent terminal commands. These patterns cause parsing failures in VS Code's terminal integration, trigger automatic retry loops under higher autonomy modes (5-10 wasted retries per failure), and bypass file-write detection guardrails. Use editor file-creation tools (create_file / editFiles) for multi-line content, and run created files with short terminal commands.

evidenceRefs.title: VS Code Agent Mode documentation — terminal tool limitations and auto-approval

publicSummary: Heredocs and inline scripts in agent terminal commands cause parsing failures amplified by auto-retry loops. Use editor file tools for multi-line content instead.
cautionNotes: Heredocs in committed scripts (not agent terminal commands) are a different concern — they should be refactored to template files but are not an immediate agent-workflow failure risk.
nonGeneralizationNotes: This specifically addresses AI agent terminal tool invocations, not general shell scripting best practices.
